nodes.description: Antimycobacterial therapy is effective for early lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Buruli ulcer: a review of in vitro tests to screen natural products for activity against Mycobacterium ulcerans.
evidence.snippet: While antimycobacterial therapy is often effective for the earliest nodular or ulcerative lesions

nodes.description: Mycobacterium ulcerans depends on DNA-dependent RNA polymerase for transcription. Rifampicin, the backbone of WHO-recommended antimycobacterial therapy (with clarithromycin or streptomycin), binds the RpoB beta subunit and blocks RNA elongation; because single rpoB mutations confer high-level resistance, it is given in combination.

evidence.snippet: The most clinically important and extensively studied class of antibiotics known to inhibit bacterial RNAP are the rifamycins.
